action.reasoning: Observation: The k-NN classifier with flipped training features and score-level TTA achieved a new best of 86.41% average accuracy on iteration 26. Analysis: Because the IN21k ViT backbone is frozen, we can further boost accuracy by training a lightweight linear head on the full cached feature buffer (90k samples) after all tasks are collected. This avoids continual-learning optimization instabilities entirely. A linear probe on 768-d features with 900 samples/class should be very strong and can be ensembled with the existing k-NN classifier for a further gain. Decision: Write train_head.py to train a linear head on the cached buffer with AdamW + cosine annealing + label smoothing, evaluate on val, search the best ensemble weight between k-NN and linear head, update predict.py to support the ensemble, verify offline, and immediately submit eval in the same iteration.
